question: Will any of the Magnificent 7 make an acquisition valued at $10B or more before September 1, 2026?

factors.description: Recent hot regime rate ~2.5-3/yr Mag7 $10B+ signings; Poisson over 2-month window centers ~0.34-0.39
Record 2026 M&A environment: megadeals +52% YoY, permissive Trump antitrust, huge free cash flow, AI arms race, 7 companies as potential acquirers
Big Tech acquisition counts at decade low; giants prioritize infrastructure capex ($725B) and minority AI stakes over $10B+ controlling buyouts; large tech deals on antitrust watchlist
No specific pending/rumored $10B+ Mag7 controlling deal expected to be signed in Jul-Aug 2026 (Amazon-Globalstar already signed April, pre-window; Wiz/Groq closed pre-window)

summary: This is a forward-looking rate question over a ~2-month window (Jul 1 – Aug 31, 2026) requiring a NEW definitive agreement for a $10B+ controlling acquisition by one of the Mag 7. The three big recent Mag7 megadeals (Amazon-Globalstar $11.6B signed April, Google-Wiz $32B, Nvidia-Groq $20B) were all signed/closed before the window, so they don't count. No specific deal is publicly expected to sign in the window. Estimating from the recent hot-regime rate (~2.5-3 such signings/yr) via a Poisson model gives P(≥1) ≈ 0.34-0.39. The record-breaking 2026 M&A environment and permissive antitrust push toward the upper end, while decade-low Big Tech deal counts and a capex-over-buyouts focus temper it. Final estimate ≈ 0.37.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.37 (37%)**

This question asks whether any Magnificent 7 company will sign a *new* definitive agreement for a $10B+ controlling acquisition during the ~2-month window (July 1 – Aug 31, 2026).

Key reasoning:
- **The big 2026 megadeals don't count** — Amazon-Globalstar ($11.6B, signed April), Google-Wiz ($32B), and Nvidia-Groq ($20B) were all signed/closed *before* the window opened.
- **No specific in-window deal is pending** — research found no rumored $10B+ Mag7 controlling acquisition expected to sign in Jul-Aug 2026.
- **Base rate:** The recent hot regime produces ~2.5-3 such Mag7 signings/year. A Poisson model over 2 months gives P(≥1) ≈ 0.34-0.39.
- **Environment tilts up slightly:** Record 2026 M&A pace, permissive Trump antitrust, and huge cash piles across 7 potential acquirers, but tempered by decade-low Big Tech deal counts and a preference for infrastructure capex/minority stakes over large buyouts.

Final: **0.37**.
